title: myosotis pygmaea var. traillii
intro: Myosotis pygmaea var. traillii, also known as Dwarf Forget-me-not, is a small perennial plant with blue flowers that grows in wetland areas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it a potential treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of the plant include relief from congestion, bronchial spasms, and coughs, as well as its potential to act as a diuretic. The herbal preparations of Myosotis pygmaea var. traillii include infusions and tinctures, which are typically made from the leaves and flowers of the plant.
